title: was part of the morning crew working onThe Pitt‘s third season this morning
full_text: Skip to main content By Rosy Cordero Rosy Cordero Associate Editor, TV @SocialRosy More Stories By Rosy Shawn Hatosy Says His ‘The Pitt’ Character Is “Living On Borrowed Time,” Reveals He Will Direct Episode Of Season 3—Emmys Noah Wyle Celebrates ‘The Pitt’ Emmy Noms & Says Season 3 Will Highlight “How Desperate This Particular Moment In Time Is” ‘The Pitt’ Stars Noah Wyle, Katherine LaNasa & Shawn Hatosy Lead Emmy Acting Nods As Medical Drama Racks Up 25 Nominations—Cast Reacts View All July 8, 2026 12:29pm Katherine LaNasa, Noah Wyle, Patrick Ball and Sepideh Moafi in 'The Pitt' Season 2. Warrick Page/HBOMAX HBO Max ‘s highly acclaimed medical drama The Pitt beat their 2025 total Emmy nominations of 13 for Season 1 with a total of 25 nominations for Season 2, with a heavy emphasis on acting nods. The cast was on set working hard on Season 3 as the nominations were announced this morning. During a quick break, creator, writer and executive producer R. Scott Gemmill announced the list of nominees, creating much excitement in the fictional Pittsburgh Trauma Medical Center, which proudly shoots in Los Angeles. “It’s kind of perfect that today is just a workday for us. We had a 7 am crew call. Most of the actors got called between 5:30 and 6:30 for makeup. I was not in the first scene. I got here, changed my clothes, went to stage, watched the first scene getting shot, and then we were starting to rehearse the second scene,” shared Noah Wyle , star, executive producer, writer, and director, about his morning. Related Stories News Jinny Howe On Netflix's Emmy Haul, 'The Diplomat' & 'Beef' Bumps, What's Next For Nominated Series & Streamer's Prestige Push With String Of Big Buys News Shailene Woodley Talks 'Paradise' Emmy Nomination, "No Drama" Work Environments & Teases 'Count My Lies' Adaptation He continued, “Once we were ready to go, we invited the crew in to watch it. They walked in; everyone was smiling. They had been outside with their phones. We did a rehearsal, and then we gave the crew the set. As we were setting up that first shot, all the writers came over from the office, and Scott came down. Before anybody had had a chance to look at their phones or hear anything, Scott read the nominations in front of all of us: cast, crew, background. We got to hear it in real time together, which was really amazing. We all jumped around and hugged each other for about 10 minutes.” Watch on Deadline More from my chat with Wyle can be found here. For a second year in a row, The Pitt has been nominated for Best Drama Series, and will have strong competition from Netflix’s The Diplomat , HBO’s The Gilded Age and Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Hulu’s Paradise, Apple TV’s Slow Horses , Pluribus, and Your Friends and Neighbors . Wyle was nominated for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series and for Outstanding Directing in a Drama Series for Episode 6 “12:00 P.M.” Wyle won the award for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series for Season 1 of The Pitt. Shawn Hatosy, who plays Dr. Jack Abbot, received a nomination for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series this morning, after changing categories. He took home Outstanding Guest Actor in a Drama Series for Season 1 last year, his first Emmy. Learn what’s ahead for Abbot in Season 3 from Shawn Hatosy in our interview here. Dr. Al-Hashimi (Spideh Moafi) plays catcher to obtain a sample from the superbaby Jane Doe with the help of Dr. Robby (Noah Wyle) and Nurse Jesse Van Horn. Warrick Page/HBO Max Under the category of Outstanding Supporting Actress in a Drama Series, the spotlighted three actresses from The Pitt: Katherine LaNasa, who last year won the Emmy in the same category, as well as nods for Sepideh Moafi, Fiona Dourif, and Taylor Dearden. Dearden’s father, Bryan Cranston, has thrice been nominated for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series for his work on Malcolm in the Middle . “I don’t know if I have words for what this moment means for me. I’m so inspired by the character. I’m so grateful she exists in the larger ecosystem of Hollywood and television. You and I have had a lot of conversations about the visibility and representation that she brings, and it’s not just about her checking a box; it’s about who she is. She’s this woman who is so high-achieving, so successful, so so capable and competent and fierce and generous and loving and caring and empathic,” Moafi, who playes Dr. Baran Al-Hashimi, shared earlier today. She added, “It’s just so fucking cool to be able to share that with everyone else and show that we are more than headlines. So, especially in this moment of darkness, we are offering hope and inspiration to so many girls around the world who carry these identities. And it’s not just about her background, her race; it’s about her health condition too. There are people who have chronic illness or disability, or any kind of chronic disease, who are often told that, ‘Your life ends here, or your life is limited.’ So, seeing a woman navigate in a very, very strict and difficult system and navigate this level of power and excellence is very inspiring.” LaNasa credits The Pitt’ s writing team for writing such “a great character” for her, Charge Nurse Dana Evans, and explains why she puts her full trust in them. “It’s just a great character. How lucky am I? It’s just a terrific character, and they write her so well. I never even ask what’s coming up next, you know what I mean? I know it’s going to be great. It’s like they seem to have locked into something, and they write material that I really love. I feel like I’m lucky enough to feel like I understand, you know? And then, gosh, you meet these nurses while doing your research, and they’re amazing people,” said LaNasa of her real-life inspiration when playing Dana. She added, “Dana is loosely based on Kathy Garvin. And some of the things she said to me when I shadowed her, I think about all the time. I hold it with me every day. It’s incredible to honor them through the role.” In addition to Hatosy, for Outstanding Supporting Actors in a Drama Series, the TV Academy recognized Patrick Ball, who plays Dr. Frank Langdon, and Gerran Howell, who stars as Dr. Dennis Whitaker. “I give all the credit to Scott, our showrunner, for writing me the most incredible part that anybody could ever dream of. I’ve been incredibly excited to be part of this project that has reached so many people, and that means so much to many,” said Ball. He continued, “To be given this part strikes really close to home. I come from a family of healthcare workers. I have a lot of experience with addiction and recovery. To have my big first TV show telling both the story of the people that I come from and those fighting the same kind of fight as me is not something you can plan. I don’t think I did anything to deserve it this way, but I just think the universe has really, really blessed me. I am so grateful.” Three guest actresses were nominated for Emmys : Tal Anderson, who plays Dearden’s character Mel’s twin sister, Brittany Allen, who played cancer patient Roxie, and Tina Ivlev, who worked closely alongside LaNasa’s Dana Evans in the impactful and important episode about a sexual assault survivor (Ivlev) and her caring nurse (LaNasa). RELATE D : ‘The Pitt’ Actress Tal Anderson Talks Becca’s New Boyfriend In Season 2, Respecting The Body Autonomy Of Disabled People & Autism Awareness Month Book Release Anderson, who is autistic, feels extremely proud of her portrayal of Becca, who is also autistic, and of the excitement there’s been among fans, many of whom feel seen through this character. “The recognition means everything to me as an actor, but especially as a disabled actor, to be recognized for portraying a storyline that, at its heart, gives disabled adults a voice about subjects that have never been talked about openly by most people. That pretty much means everything to me,” Anderson said. She continued, “This recognition for the disabled community feels like a shift, really. It feels like maybe it will be possible for authentic disabled stories to be told more often. I hope that this is the case, and I really hope we get to see and hear more of those authentic stories. Going forward, I hope it will be more common for those stories to be written and portrayed authentically. The Pitt is really doing a good thing for a lot of underrepresented communities, and my disabled community is one of them.” Wyle noted the importance of the Becca and Mel storyline: “Tal represents our show beautifully. Yes, as well as representing herself and people who are neurodivergent. I love listening to her be interviewed because she’s so honest and articulate and has a way of framing the experience from her point of view that is totally singular. I was overjoyed to see that she was nominated because her work is fantastic. It really is beautiful.” He continued, “And that relationship between Taylor and Tal is one of the cornerstones of our show. The sister relationship, Mel’s investment in that relationship, her sort of need to navigate the new dynamics now that Becca’s got some independence and some love in her life, are a big part of Taylor’s arc for Season 3.” Anderson stayed mum about what fans could expect to see from Becca next season, saying she’s not even sure if she will appear yet. However, as anxious as viewers are to see the character’s boyfriend reveal, so is she. Tal Anderson and Taylor Dearden on ‘The Pitt’ Season 2. HBO Max Outstanding Guest Actor in a Drama Series nominees are Ernest Harden, Jr., who played Louie Cloverfield, and Jeff Kober, who played Dr. Robby’s longtime friend Duke Ekins. Ball, who shared many scenes with Harden Jr., reflected on their characters’ relationship and touts why he is so proud of the actor’s nomination. “Louie is incredible, and Ernest is too. I remember in Season 1, our first scene with him, he comes in on a gurney. Me and Santos [Isa Briones] are there, and we’re trying to get him sorted and medicated. The whole scene is really about Santos suspecting me, but the entire time I am just looking at Ernest, and I’m just absolutely blown away by his charisma. He’s just this unbelievable bright light; I was so charmed by him. I had absolutely no idea the importance that he was going to play in Langdon’s arc and his journey, and certainly didn’t know he would be coming back in Season 2,” Ball shared. Adding, “In fact, Louie is the first person that Langdon runs into whenever he walks into work. I had no idea where [the story] was going, but I was absolutely blown away by his presence. I think Louie and Langdon are very much kindred spirits.” Langdon (Patrick Ball) tends to Louie (Ernest Harden, Jr.) and has a heart to heart w/him in ‘The Pitt’ Warrick Page/HBO Max LaNasa also honored her co-star Ivlev for her nomination today. The two shared scenes in Episode 7, titled “1:00 P.M.” “I love that! It was so exciting. I actually just walked off the stage, and I haven’t talked to anybody. I haven’t even seen the list, but they did read the list for us. We don’t have phones on set. That’s super exciting for Tina. Can’t wait to get in touch with her. She so deserves it, and she was so wonderful in those scenes. It’s what made the episode terrific. She’s really one of the finest actors I’ve ever worked with.” Additional nominations include: Outstanding Production Design For A Narrative Contemporary Program (One Hour or More) for Nina Ruscio, Production Designer, Josh Lusby, Art Director, and Matt Callahan, Set Decorator. Outstanding Casting For a Drama Series for Cathy Sandrich Gelfond, Erica Berger, and Seth Caskey, Associate Casting Director Outstanding Picture Editing for a Drama Series for Mark Strand Outstanding Picture Editing for a Drama Series for Tamara Luciano Outstanding Contemporary Makeup (NON-PROSTHETIC) for Merry Lee Traum, Key Makeup Artist and makeup artists Marie-Flore ‘Ri’ Beaubien and Leesa Simone. Outstanding Prosthetic Makeup for Myriam Arougheti, Department Head Makeup Artist, Arjen Tuiten, Prosthetic Designer, Thom Floutz, Special Makeup Effects Artist, Chris Burgoyne, Special Makeup Effects Artist, Martina Sykes, Special Makeup Effects Artist and Hanny Tjan, Special Makeup Effects Artist. Outstanding Original Music and Lyrics for Gavin Brivik and Andrew Bird Outstanding Sound Mixing for a Comedy or Drama Series (One Hour) for Todd Grace, Re-Recording Mixer, Edward C. Carr, III, Re-Recording Mixer, Von Varga, Production Mixer, Tami Treadwell, ADR Mixer, and Alex Jongbloed, CAS, Foley Mixer. Outstanding Writing for a Drama Series for Kirsten “Cookie” Pierre-Geyfman and R. title: broke the news
full_text: Skip to main content By Nellie Andreeva Nellie Andreeva Editor-In-Chief @DeadlineNellie More Stories By Nellie ‘It’s Not Like That’: Scott Foley Notes Series’ Strong Reviews & Performance As “Disappointed But Grateful” Star Laments Cancellation By Prime Video Netflix Series Chief On ‘I Will Find You’ Success, The “Sophomore Slump” Phenomenon, ‘The Boroughs’ Cancellation & ‘Virgin River’s Future Jinny Howe On Netflix’s Emmy Haul, ‘The Diplomat’ & ‘Beef’ Bumps, What’s Next For Nominated Series & Streamer’s Prestige Push With String Of Big Buys View All July 7, 2026 4:30pm 'It's Not Like That' Steve Dietl/Amazon MGM Studios EXCLUSIVE : It’s Not Like That ‘s Rotten Tomatoes score will stay at a perfect 100%, and the series’ leads Malcolm (Scott Foley) and Lori (Erinn Hayes) will remain star-crossed lovers. That is because Wonder Project’s faith-based family drama will not be getting a second season by Prime Video , Deadline has learned. Related Stories Cancellations and Renewals 2026 TV Cancellations Photo Gallery: Series Ending This Year & Beyond Cancellations and Renewals 2026 TV Series Renewals Photo Gallery The news comes more than a month and a half after It’s Not Like That was released globally on Prime Video. The series is part of the streamer’s ongoing collaboration with Wonder Project, which also is behind House of David. Both faith-based dramas launch exclusively on Wonder Project’s Prime Video add-on subscription channel first; It’s Not Like That debuted on the service January 25 before getting a wide release on Prime Video on May 15. House of David , whose second season premiered on the Wonder Project add-on in November and on Prime Video in late March, is getting a third season, I hear. Watch on Deadline

title: Sky announced
full_text: Skip to main content By Jake Kanter , Jesse Whittock July 5, 2026 11:08pm ITV and Sky will combine to create a UK broadcasting giant. ITV/Sky Sky has finally sealed a £1.6 billion ($2.1 billion) deal to acquire ITV ‘s television network operations. ITV confirmed to shareholders on Monday morning that it will sell to its pay-TV rival, meaning a crown jewel of British broadcasting becomes part of the NBCUniversal entertainment empire. Related Stories News Job Cuts, Content Sharing, Exec Rejigs & Protracted Antitrust Scrutiny: Five Things You Need To Know About Sky’s “Historic” Takeover Of ITV News Sky Chief Dana Strong Says ITV Will Remain “Deeply British” As Hollywood Tightens Grip On UK Media As the Paramount -Warner Bros. Discovery merger inches towards completion, Sky’s acquisition of ITV is another sign that traditional television companies are turning to seismic consolidation to compete with the likes of Netflix and YouTube. The agreement follows a forensic courtship that has lasted nearly nine months after ITV first notified the London Stock Exchange in November that its media and entertainment division was under offer.
